2 Triangle is rotated about the origin and then reected over the axis to form triangle . One of the angles in triangle has a measure of . One of the angles in triangle has a measure of . Which is the measure of an angle in triangle ? A B C D What is the value of in the equation ? A B C D

5 Two electricians, Electrician A and Electrician B, offer pricing plans for their work. Each electrician charges an initial fee for a service call plus an hourly rate. The charges for each electrician are represented by the equation and the table shown below. Which statement comparing the charges for each electrician is true? A Electrician A has an initial fee and an hourly rate that are both less than those for Electrician B. B Electrician A has an initial fee and an hourly rate that are both greater than those for Electrician B. C Electrician A has an initial fee that is less than that for Electrician B. The hourly rate for Electrician A is greater than that for Electrician B. D Electrician A has an initial fee that is greater than that for Electrician B. The hourly rate for Electrician A is less than that for Electrician B.

A sphere has a radius of inches. A cone has a radius of inches and a height of inches. Which expression represents the difference in volume, in cubic inches, between the sphere and the cone? A B C D

The scatter plot below shows the weight, in pounds, of an alligator as it grows at a zoo. The line has been drawn to best represent the relationship between the alligator’s weight and growth time. Which statement best describes what the number in the equation represents in this situation? A The starting weight, in pounds, of the alligator. B The approximate number of pounds the alligator gains each week. C The maximum number of pounds the alligator gains each week. D The average number of pounds of food the alligator is fed each week.

Trapezoid ABCD and its image A’B’C’D’ are shown on the coordinate plane below. Which series of transformations could be applied to map trapezoid ABCD onto trapezoid A’B’C’D’ ? A reection over the x axis and then a rotation of 180˚ about the origin B reection over the x axis and then a rotation of 90˚ about the origin C reection over the y axis and then a rotation of 180˚ about the origin D reection over the y axis and then a rotation of 90˚ about the origin Which equation represents a linear function? A B C D

20 On a coordinate plane, triangle is rotated degrees clockwise about the origin and then dilated with a scale factor of centered at the origin to form triangle . Which statement describes the relationship between triangle and triangle ? A They are similar and congruent. B They are similar but not congruent. C They are congruent but not similar. D They are neither congruent nor similar. One of the angles in a triangle measures degrees. Another angle in the triangle measures degrees. Which expression represents the measurement, in degrees, of the third angle of the triangle? A B C D

T ake your time. • You have a ruler , a protractor, a reference sheet, and a calculator that you can use on the test if they help you answer the question. • Be sure to show your work when asked. • Be sure to explain your answer when asked. The table below represents a function. Which statement describes the function? A The function is nonlinear because the intercept is 0. B The function is linear because the rate of change is constant. C The function is linear because the intercept is a constant value. D The function is nonlinear because the rate of change is not constant.
Quadrilateral is rotated about the origin to form quadrilateral . Which two sides are equal in length? A and B and C and D and

A manufacturer makes cylindrical cans in two sizes. The dimensions of each can are shown in the diagram below. What is the difference between the volumes, in cubic centimeters, of the large can and the small can in terms of ? A B C D An equation is shown below. Which statement about the equation is true? A It has no solutions. B It has exactly one solution. C It has exactly two solutions. D It has an innite number of solutions.

38 This question is worth 1 credit. A movie theater sells popcorn in cone shaped containers as shown below. What is the volume, in cubic inches, of the popcorn container? Round your answer to the nearest tenth. Answer cubic inches
This question is worth 1 credit. The area of a square shaped garden is 324 square feet. What is the length, in feet, of each side of the garden? Answer feet
This question is worth 1 credit. Line segment AB is graphed on the coordinate plane shown below. What is the length, in units, of line segment AB ? Answer units
This question is worth 2 credits. In the gure below, line p is parallel to line q and lines and are transversals. What is the measure, in degrees, of ? Show your work. Answer degrees

This question is worth 2 credits. The equation is used to model the yearly salary, , of an employee, in thousands of dollars, where is the number of years the employee has worked for the company. What does the slope of the line represent in this situation? Explain your answer.

This question is worth 2 credits. A dog owner collected data to see which of his two dogs runs at the greater speed. The graph and the table below show the relationship between the time, in seconds, and the distance, in feet, each dog ran. What is the difference, in feet per second, between the speeds of the two dogs? Show your work. Answer feet per second
This question is worth 2 credits. Two ordered pairs of a linear function are shown below. What is the rate of change for the function? Show your work. Answer
This question is worth 2 credits. What value of makes the equation shown below true? Show your work. Answer
This question is worth 2 credits. A list of numbers is shown below. Classify each number as either rational or irrational. Be sure to include how you know a number is rational. Explain your answer.

This question is worth 3 credits. Store A and Store B sell fabric for different prices. The equation represents the price, , in dollars, for yards of fabric at Store A. The graph below represents the price for the same type of fabric at Store B. What is the unit rate for the price of fabric, per yard, at each store? Store A $ per yard of fabric Store B $ per yard of fabric How much more would the price of yards of fabric be at Store A than at Store B ? Show your work. Answer $
